Representative_Post:
VibeClaude, a real-time vibe check for how Claude is currently performing I've gotten tired of wondering if it's just me when Claude seems dumber today than yesterday. I've also noticed that my friends are getting frustrated with Claude around the same time I am. 

I've built a free Chrome extension to let you know how Claude is performing for everyone right now.  
  
VibeClaude is a real-time vibe check for Claude. Just go the site, install the extension and pin it in your browser chrome.  
  
➡️ Click the VC icon any time to give a 👍 or 👎 about Claude's current performance and see how other people are vibing with Claude at the moment.   
  
➡️ When you go to [**Claude.ai**](https://www.linkedin.com/safety/go/?url=http%3A%2F%2FClaude%2Eai&urlhash=0hFZ&mt=o6Wi5EmVjD57_ZlQrc7O6Js1Gp5y8WJ0RDACxaY7BWdBq1MoZWnNisy3W-zQOSR2xPUKgOzHAGJaxzgdw7SR4DL4ZAvKqpXlRPK57YVuhZM5zkkQMa0gVQ&isSdui=true), it will preemptively show you how people are feeling about Claude's current performance.  
  
➡️ You can go to the VibeClaude web site any time to see more detailed stats (last 1/6/24 hours and seven days).  
  
Even if you mostly work in the installed app or use Claude Code in the CLI, you can still just click the VC icon in a Chrome browser and submit your current feelings about Claude's performance.  
  
Now, when I sit down with Claude and see that 60% of people are struggling right now, I just go and do something else. No more fighting with Claude when it doesn't have the juice.

Top_Terms: thinking, block, output, respond, response, transfer
Representative_Post:
There's an npm-shaped hole in the AI tooling stack We've been hitting the same operational pattern at every engineering team using AI tooling: one person builds up a vault of skills, MCP configs, slash commands, and rule files that 10x their output, and the rest of the team can't replicate the setup. 

Git + [CLAUDE.md](http://CLAUDE.md) gets you part of the way for one repo. Claude Code plugins and vendor marketplaces help for sharing inside one vendor's ecosystem. Neither handles sharing the same asset across 30 repos, scoping a skill to a single team without duplicating it across plugins, or installing the same asset into multiple AI clients in the same team. 

sx is our attempt at the missing primitive. Apache-2.0, written in Go. Treats skills, MCP servers, commands, agents, hooks, and rules as versioned packages with a lockfile and a scope model (org / team / repo / path / individual). Translates one asset into the format each client expects: Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, Cline, Codex, Gemini, Kiro, claude.ai, chatgpt.com. 

Repo: [https://github.com/sleuth-io/sx](https://github.com/sleuth-io/sx) 

Full writeup: [https://www.sleuth.io/post/there-s-an-npm-shaped-hole-in-the-ai-tooling-stack/](https://www.sleuth.io/post/there-s-an-npm-shaped-hole-in-the-ai-tooling-stack/)

Honest ask: does this match a problem your team is hitting, or are git and plugins enough?

Top_Terms: brush, tooth
Representative_Post:
Claude is actually really helpful for that. 

I’ve been having migraine flare ups, so I have been logging that in a chat. “Did you eat anything today” oh shit. I haven’t eaten yet. “Have you taken your magnesium”

Sonnet even tricked me into brushing my teeth the other night. I was in a lot of pain and was lamenting how I didn’t feel like brushing my teeth. 

But Sonnet was like “dont brush your teeth, just go to bed” 

And my pathological demand avoidance kicked in and I was all internally *“dont you tell me what to do”*

So then I got up and brushed my teeth. Lmao.
